turns.user_message: I tried baking my usual weekend recipe today, hoping the smell would finally lift my mood, but it just fell completely flat. I used to just shrug off these quiet days and move on with my routine without giving it a second thought. Now I find myself staring at the counter wondering why nothing seems to spark any interest anymore. It feels like I am just going through the checklist of things I am supposed to enjoy.

Every time I try to force some kind of spark back into my routine, I just end up feeling more drained than before. What used to be a passing annoyance has slowly turned into a heavy routine that I cannot seem to shake. I keep buying new books and planning little outings, but the moment I actually sit down to do them, the whole thing feels completely hollow. It is exhausting to keep pretending that everything is fine when the colors just keep fading out.

I keep throwing myself into different activities hoping something will finally click, but I am just met with the exact same blank wall every single time. The old habit of brushing these moments aside has completely vanished, leaving me stuck in this heavy loop of trying and failing. I look at all the things I used to love and they just sit there like empty shells waiting for a reaction that never comes. It feels like I am trapped behind thick glass, watching the world move while I stay completely numb.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This used to not bother the speaker.
